_meta.note: SINGLE source of truth for format codes. Scripts MUST reference this file and never duplicate the code array. SE is an assay OVERLAY, not a base format. MUSH is an ingredient class, not a format. IC/LP route to application review, not a physical base.

RE.description: Dry full-spectrum extract powder; solubility unknown from Type, water-favouring.

SD.description: Dry spray-dried powder; water-dispersibility is SKU-dependent, not proven by Type.

FD.description: Freeze-dried whole-material powder (parallel to MP/SD). A freeze-dried EXTRACT is NOT FD: model as its base extract code with a freeze-dry process note.

WL-glycol.description: Supplier-declared liquid extract in propylene glycol. Distinct from generic WL (regulatory/clean-label suitability varies).

WL-glycerin.description: Supplier-declared liquid extract in glycerin. Distinct from generic WL (carrier identity affects formulation/labelling).

RE-paste.description: Semi-solid extract state; behaviour depends on carrier/solids/phase/processing.

OLR.description: Concentrated resinous extract; flavour/colour/oil-phase associations. Phase/standardisation SKU-specific.

HYD.description: Aqueous distillate with volatile constituents. Not an essential oil, WL, or ordinary liquid extract.

EO.description: Steam-distilled / expressed volatile aromatic oil. Distinct from carrier oil (CO), oil-soluble extract (OE) and hydrosol (HYD). Not a solubility/behaviour claim.

SE.description: Assay overlay: standardised to a quantified marker. NEVER a base physical format; always sits on a base. Carries {marker, assay, method}.

IC.description: Isolate / high-purity active. Routes to application review (§6). Not a physical base.

LP.description: Liposomal delivery. Routes to application review (§6). Not a physical base.

mushroom.label: Mushroom ingredient
mushroom.note: Ingredient CLASS, not a format. Record ingredient_class=mushroom PLUS a real base format (MP/RE/WD/etc.). MUSH must never be a base code.

not_a_botanical_format.note: Supplier Type values in these families are NOT botanical commercial formats and must go to unmapped_types (do not guess): essential oil, fragrance/aroma/diffuser oil, finished dosage (capsules/tablets/gummies), cosmetics/skin-care finished goods, peptides/synthetic actives, clays/colorants/minerals, foods/beverages-finished, enzymes/probiotics.
